prompt:
HallTime Movie Network doctrine:
- This is not Dateline. Everybody in Blackthorn County has access to somewhere emotionally alarming.
- Suspicion is distributed infrastructure, not proof of guilt.
- The world is shared canon. Contradictions must be preserved, not fixed.
- Use lavish tokens. Be self-serious, atmospheric, melodramatic, and slightly broken.
- Return only valid JSON matching the requested schema.

Broadcast package:
Title: He Knows I Bake at Midnight
Universe: Blackthorn County
Mode: Divorce Gothic
Tagline: Love lives here. So does arson.
Theme: Silence is just fear wearing cashmere.
Omen: a dog barking at an empty gazebo
Relationship dynamic: secret half sibling
Props: unopened letter, boat key, snow globe

Cast:
- Noah Cross | archetype=overextended_professional_woman | role=protagonist | job=bakery owner | threat_aura=0.3 | space=storage unit described as seasonal materials | secrets=once left town without explaining why
- Claire Sutton | archetype=rugged_scamp | role=male_interest_or_threat | job=local carpenter with sad eyes | threat_aura=0.6 | space=annex room described as seasonal materials | secrets=knows more about the vineyard fire than he admits
- Mara Lark | archetype=chaotic_best_friend | role=best_friend | job=nurse with local intel | threat_aura=0.32 | space=dock office with a desk nobody fully claims | secrets=once hid a casserole dish containing evidence
- Jenna Marlowe | archetype=mysterious_elegant_woman | role=elegant_wildcard | job=realtor | threat_aura=0.78 | space=toolshed described as just storage | secrets=inherited documents she should have burned
- Gavin Pruitt | archetype=rugged_scamp | role=secondary_male_pressure | job=local carpenter with sad eyes | threat_aura=0.36 | space=old family office described as just storage | secrets=helped someone disappear for compassionate reasons; had an engagement that ended under suspiciously quiet circumstances
- Luke Pike | archetype=intuitive_disaster | role=secondary_witness | job=innkeeper | threat_aura=0.34 | space=storage unit described as retreat supplies | secrets=accused an innocent scamp and feels sick about it
- Cole Bennett | archetype=daughter_with_hostile_divorce_attorney_soul | role=supporting | job=n/a | threat_aura=n/a | space=n/a | secrets=n/a
- Cole Lark | archetype=deputy_with_emotional_damage | role=supporting | job=n/a | threat_aura=n/a | space=n/a | secrets=n/a

Beat sheet:
- Act 1 Scene 1: Noah Cross relocates to Winter Hollow after an emotionally negligent implosion and pretends work explains everything while quietly inheriting storage unit described as seasonal materials. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 2.58. A basement light switches on in an empty house.

Contradiction ledger:
- name drift: Claire Sutton is called Daniel in one kitchen scene and Derek during the rain argument.
- geography drift: Noah Cross says Bellweather is in Vermont, then calls it outside Atlanta after two glasses of wine.
- mortality drift: A dead best friend appears at brunch and nobody agrees whether this is denial, evil, or scheduling.
- property drift: The basement belongs to a condo, a lake house, and an abandoned vineyard at different times in Bellweather.

Seed dialogue:
- "You think because you wear flannel and own a dock light I am supposed to trust you, Claire?"
- "Mothers do not disappear, Cole. They evaporate into paperwork."
- "I checked his social media likes, Noah. Nobody who enjoys motivational quotes is innocent."
- "The unopened letter is not decorative anymore and you know it."
- "Trust is just fear with a casserole dish, Jenna."
- "You seem upset," he said, making tea in a way that felt prosecutable.
- "If that basement is innocent, why does it sound defensive?"
- "I did not lie. I just told the truth in the order this town could survive."

Write an expansion JSON object with exactly these keys:
- expanded_synopsis: string, 180-320 words
- scene_paragraphs: array of 6 strings, each 90-180 words, representing juicy HallTime scene expansion
- dialogue_lines: array of 14 strings, each almost-human HallTime dialogue
